rationale: The two papers share a thematic link around really new products and uncertainty/market research, which makes a non-incidental citation plausible. However, the citing paper is primarily a game-theoretic entrant–incumbent model, while the cited paper is an empirical conjoint/measurement study, and the only matched evidence is a single DOI reference with no in-text context. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
